criteria_checked.note: CONFIRMED order-independent for VALIDATION: split_sections (:399-426) builds a dict keyed by section number; validate_doc (:444-469) iterates SECTIONS by number and looks up by key — physical position is irrelevant. A2 reorder keeping numeric labels (## 7./## 9.) PASSES `agents-md --validate`. RENUMBERING would be rejected by the title-to-number pin (SECTIONS :60-71). Plan correct on the validator.

REFUTED for the module SELFTEST. The plan's finding that generator selftests are 'all order-independent' overlooks the negative-control FIXTURE regexes INSIDE selftest(): V1 (:683) and V4 (:697-698) hardcode physical §6→§7 and §9→§10 adjacency. Empirically reproduced (see B1). This is the load-bearing defect.

CONFIRMED: dmc-v0.4.7-context-audit.sh:52 greps AGENTS.md for 'AUTONOMY.md' AND 'CONTEXT_MAP.md'; both strings live in §7's companion-docs paragraph (generator :322-327), gated on companion_docs_present(root) (all three of AUTONOMY.md/docs/CONTEXT_MAP.md/docs/DMC_CONSTITUTION.md at root). A2 moves §7's POSITION but not its bytes, so AC6 survives — PROVIDED regeneration runs at repo root so the gated paragraph renders (advisory A-adv4). AC6 criterion + audit run cover a miss.

CONFIRMED: no consumer parses §4/§5 CONTENT. repo-intel.py:213 lists AGENTS.md in a filename tuple (scan/existence); context-budgeter:49,155 treats it as project-memory with a static lines:60 estimate (filename only); doctor.py:225 os.path.isfile; v0.1.3-verify.sh:47 existence. The only content readers are AC6's §7 grep (protected) and context-audit AC4 `wc -l` bloat bound (400) — post-dedup AGENTS.md stays ~286 lines (the 8,489 B blob is ONE line) < 400. No tool HASHES AGENTS.md. Dedup+reorder are consumer-safe.

CONFIRMED bin/lib/dmc-agents-md.py is NOT in the gate's DEFAULT_PROTECTED (dmc-v0.2.6-gate-check-runner.sh:22-31 = 10 worker/provider/hooks/schema paths; grep count 0). So NO DMC_GATE_PROTECTED override is needed — the plan's conditional resolves to 'no override' (advisory A-adv2). The plan HEDGES ('if that path is in DEFAULT_PROTECTED') so it does not over-claim. The enforcement-class landmark-FLAG is a SEPARATE non-suppressible mechanism and will still rise as the plan expects.

The proposed §5⊆§4 subset guard is a TAUTOLOGY, not a sufficient guard. protected_landmarks (§5) is DERIVED as a filtered subset of §4 landmarks at :215-218, so §5⊆§4 is true by construction and can only fail if the derivation is refactored — it does NOT guard the RENDER dedup (:303-306), which is where the plan's change lives. Separately: the dedup CANNOT silently drop a protected surface regardless of the guard, because every §5 path remains in §4 with its class label. Recommend a COUNT-parity guard (rendered §5 per-class counts == derived per-class counts of §4 enforcement/contract/release) to actually catch an off-by-one / mislabeled count. Advisory A-adv1 — the change is safe either way; not a blocker.

CONFIRMED II.2 802/3/3 is NOT perturbed: dmc-legacy-selftest.py PINNED_BASELINE {tools:49, pass:802, fail:3, na:3} covers ONLY the 49 pre-v1.0 dmc-v0.* tools (NOFLAG_TOOLS+SELFTEST_TOOLS+PY_CORES); dmc-agents-md.py is an M6.5 native module, NOT in that set — so adding a module-selftest assertion does NOT bump 802, and editing the file does NOT break mirror-check (55-file dmc-v0.* byte-equality set). AC '802/3/3 EXACT' is achievable and non-contradictory. Bucket-A holds: change touches only the generator render/emit-order + a selftest assertion + regenerated artifact + MILESTONES — no floor/gate/when-gates-fire/schema. IV.2 lexeme discipline not in play: §7 Codex-lexeme prose is moved, not edited (bytes unchanged). No frozen hash of AGENTS.md exists (m8 hashes .codex/config.toml).

FILE scope is adequate (the V1/V4 fix lives inside dmc-agents-md.py, already listed editable), but WORK scope is INCOMPLETE: Proposed Changes and Execution Tasks (DMC-T001..T003) enumerate only the §5 dedup, the reorder, and a NEW subset-parity assertion — they do NOT include the MANDATORY fix of the existing V1/V4 negative-control regexes that A2 breaks. The plan's Risk row 'Physical reorder trips a selftest assuming numeric file order → validator is order-independent' MISDIAGNOSES the failure locus (the break is in the test's own fixture-construction regexes, not the validator). This under-specification is B1.

ACs are objective and machine-checkable (byte margin <28672 [32,490→~24,001 after removing the confirmed 8,489 B blob on AGENTS.md:226]; grep -b §7<§4 & §9<§4; --validate VALID; AC6 grep+audit; artifact==stdout byte-identity; selftest --all 802/3/3 EXACT). The ACs are in fact STRONG ENOUGH to CATCH B1: 'module selftest 0 FAIL' and 'm6.5 agents-md suite green' would both FAIL at build under the current plan — meaning the defect is not silent, but the plan would either stall on its own AC or force an undisclosed V1/V4 edit (Constitution VIII anti-patchwork). Fix the plan, not the AC.

blockers.evidence_ref: bin/lib/dmc-agents-md.py:683,697-698 ; tests/fixtures/m6.5/test-agents-md.sh:182 ; empirical repro: reordered order [1,2,3,7,9,4,5,6,8,10] → V1 removes §6 = False, V4 leaves sections [1,2,3,7,9,10]
